MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ONEBEACON INSURANCE COMPANY'S, F.K.A. GENERAL ACCIDENT, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: PATRICIA LAKNE, ESQUIRE (DAVIDOVITZ & BENNETT, 415.956.4800) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES E. GIBBONS, ESQUIRE (MANNING & MARDER, 213.624.6900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The cross motions are denied. There are triable issues of fact that including whether the evidence presented by the parties conclusively establishes that Pankow, alone, was responsible for the injury (P8; P9 based on reliance on inadmissible the Admissions); the circumstances surrounding the tender (P17, 18 & 19); the coverage for a specific location during a specific period (P30, 32, 37); and the interplay and meaning of various parts of the policy (P38, 39, 40, 49). These factual disputes and the inferences therefrom preclude either motion from being granted. The Court's rulings on the evidence objections are as follows: Pankow's objections of Onebeacon's evidence in support - overruled objections to Lasker Declaration 1, 2, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11; sustained 3, 4 and 5; Harding Declaration overruled 1 and 2; sustained 3; Roopnarine Declaration overruled 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6; Zirpoli Declaration 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5; and Neubauer Declaration overruled 1. Pankow objections to Onebeacon evidence in opposition overruled 1, 5, 7, 8 and 9; sustained 2, 3, 4, and 6; and plaintiff's objections to evidence in defendant's opposition overruled 3; sustained 1 and 2. Judicial notice is taken of the Register of Actions in case 02-403697. MS. LAKNER IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: DIANA CHEN, CSR 11312

These factual disputes and the inferences therefrom preclude either motion from being granted. The Court's rulings on the evidence objections are as follows: Pankow's objections of Onebeacon's evidence in support - overruled objections to Lasker Declaration 1, 2, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11; sustained 3, 4 and 5; Harding Declaration overruled 1 and 2; sustained 3; Roopnarine Declaration overruled 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6; Zirpoli Declaration 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5; and Neubauer Declaration overruled 1. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Served On Lubmermens Insurance Company
Off Calendar Sep-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Patricia Lakner, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Onebeacon Insurance Company
Tony D. Shin, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Pankow Residential Builders II, LP

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Onebeacon Insurance's motion to compel compliance with subpoena served on Lubmermens Insurance company is Denied without prejudice as service of the subpoena did not comply with CCP 2020.220 (b) and 1985.3. The Court notes that it would find the subject matter of the subpoena to be relevant to the subject matter of this action if limited in scope to the period of 1998 - 2001. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
